purpose: This repository provides an AI-powered question-answering system for the CRB Lending/Selling platform, enabling natural language queries about loans, their history, interest calculations, transfers, and fees. It functions as a backend service that connects to a database containing loan information and uses Amazon Bedrock LLM APIs to process questions and generate insights.
businessFeatures: Natural language querying of loan data
Conversational chat interface with context retention

apis.purpose: Submit a question about a loan
Create or resume a chat session for a user+loan pair
Submit a user message to a session
Get all messages for a session
Stream chat responses via Server-Sent Events

dataEntities.name: Session
dataEntities.description: Represents a chat session between a user and the AI about a specific loan

dataEntities.name: Message
dataEntities.description: A message in a chat session from either the user or the assistant

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core entity representing a loan in the system with its details and status

dataEntities.name: LoanAction
dataEntities.description: Represents actions taken on a loan such as transfers or status changes

dataEntities.name: Transfer
dataEntities.description: Financial transfers related to loans such as interest payments or fees

messagingPatterns.name: Server-Sent Events
messagingPatterns.description: Streams AI responses to the client with progress updates and token-by-token delivery

architecturePatterns: Chain of Responsibility (for query processing pipeline)

findings.title: Direct SQL execution without parameterization
findings.description: The _extract_sql function in ai_service.py uses regex to extract SQL statements and executes them directly. While there are some safeguards in place (SELECT-only check), this approach could be vulnerable to SQL injection if the AI model generates malicious SQL. Consider enhancing the SQL sanitization or using a more structured approach to SQL generation.

findings.title: Database credential refresh timing issues
findings.description: In sql_executor.py, credential refresh happens outside the thread lock, which could lead to race conditions if multiple threads try to refresh credentials simultaneously. Restructure the credential refresh to happen entirely within the lock to prevent potential issues.

findings.title: Limited error handling for Bedrock API failures
findings.description: While there is retry logic for Bedrock API calls, the error messages may not provide enough details for debugging in production. Consider implementing more comprehensive error logging and fallback mechanisms when Bedrock is unavailable or experiencing high latency.
